summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements multi-layered boundary audit with snapshot-based mutation detection, scoped evidence validation, and PRD readiness gating.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() provide hash-based file mutation detection
evidence.why: Core boundary audit mechanism that detects any project file changes during agent execution

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots taken before/after agent execution with violation detection
evidence.why: Runtime implementation of boundary audit that gates PRD readiness on clean execution

evidence.summary: Boundary violations create BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that prevent PRD readiness
evidence.why: Proof that mutation detection directly gates demand readiness status

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines project_mutation as forbidden with allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Policy-level boundary declaration that controls agent tool access

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes explicit boundary rules in agent instructions
evidence.why: Agent-level boundary enforcement through prompt engineering

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION requires scope field on all evidence records
evidence.why: Schema-level requirement that prevents evidence without scope declaration

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() validates scope field and creates blockers when missing
evidence.why: Validation layer that rejects evidence without proper scope classification

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() requires file locators for project-scoped evidence
evidence.why: Prevents project facts without concrete file paths from being accepted

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles
evidence.why: Three-agent architecture that provides cross-check validation for high-risk demands

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() constructs full Claude agent invocations with tool allowlists
evidence.why: Provider adapter that enables full-tool Claude execution with configurable tool access

evidence.summary: Claude agent configuration includes allowedTools and disallowedTools parameters
evidence.why: Tool-level boundary controls that can restrict agent write access

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult() adds EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers for missing scope declarations
evidence.why: Post-processing validation that enforces schema requirements

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() orchestrates full dispatch pipeline with boundary checks
evidence.why: Main entry point that ties together snapshotting, execution, validation, and readiness gating

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() checks evidence agent results and adds blockers for incomplete agents
evidence.why: Readiness validation that requires all evidence agents to complete before PRD

evidence.summary: Final readiness check incorporates boundary blockers and updates demand status
evidence.why: Integration point where boundary audit results affect demand PRD readiness

assumptions: Full-tool Claude agents respect tool allowlist/disallowlist configuration at runtime
File hash comparison in buildBoundarySnapshot() reliably detects content changes
Git files list from spawnSync() accurately reflects tracked project files
Agent providers (Claude, Codex, custom) execute commands in the specified working directory
JSON parsing and repair logic handles malformed agent output correctly
risks: If file hash comparison fails to detect certain mutations (e.g., permission changes, timestamp-only changes), boundary violations could go undetected
If agent providers ignore tool allowlists, agents could write to project files despite boundary configuration
If boundary audit does not check symlinks or special files, mutations could bypass detection
If evidence scope validation only checks field presence and not field values, invalid scopes could be accepted

result.notes: Evidence dispatch implements comprehensive boundary audit with snapshot-based mutation detection, multi-layered scope validation, and PRD readiness gating. Architecture includes explorer/cross-checker/verifier roles for high-risk demands. Requires cross-checker and verifier verification to confirm detection efficacy and validation completeness.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements multi-layered boundary audit with snapshot-based mutation detection, scoped evidence validation, and PRD readiness gating.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() computes SHA-256 hashes for all tracked files excluding allowed_write_roots; diffBoundarySnapshots() detects added/modified/deleted files by comparing hash maps
evidence.why: Snapshot-based mutation detection mechanism that can detect any unauthorized project file changes

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshots taken before and after agent execution loop; diff changes computed and stored in result.boundary.changes
evidence.why: Runtime implementation showing actual execution-time boundary audit

evidence.summary: Boundary violations create BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers with path and change details; finalReadiness.prd_ready set to false when blockers exist
evidence.why: Direct proof that mutation detection gates PRD readiness status

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES = Set([project, external, user, unknown]); DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION requires scope field in evidence records
evidence.why: Schema-level enforcement of scope declaration requirement

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() checks VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES.has() and creates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers for missing/invalid scope values
evidence.why: Validation layer that rejects evidence without proper scope classification

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() requires file path/locator when evidence has project scope; creates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blockers
evidence.why: Prevents project facts from being accepted without concrete file evidence

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() checks evidenceResultComplete() for required roles and adds blockers for incomplete agents; calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() on complete results
evidence.why: Readiness validation that requires all evidence agents to complete with valid scoped evidence

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() called with agent_results; boundary blockers merged into readiness.blockers; result.status set to blocked when prd_ready is false
evidence.why: Integration point where boundary audit and evidence validation jointly determine PRD readiness

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker (cross-checker), and verifier roles with objectives, prompts, and result_schema
evidence.why: Three-agent architecture definition that provides cross-check validation for high-risk demands

evidence.summary: Claude args include --allowedTools and --disallowedTools parameters from config.ai; tool allowlist/disallowlist passed to agent CLI
evidence.why: Tool access controls that can restrict agent write capabilities

assumptions: SHA-256 file hashing in buildBoundarySnapshot() reliably detects content changes and is not vulnerable to collision attacks
Git ls-files command accurately reflects all tracked project files excluding excluded directories
Agent providers (Claude CLI, Codex, custom) execute in the specified working directory and respect tool allowlist/disallowlist parameters
File system operations in buildBoundarySnapshot() handle all file types correctly including symlinks and special files
risks: If SHA-256 hashing has collision vulnerabilities, different file contents could produce same hash and bypass mutation detection
If git ls-files misses certain tracked files or includes untracked files, boundary snapshot may be incomplete or include non-project files
If agent providers ignore --allowedTools/--disallowedTools parameters, agents could write to project files despite boundary configuration
If boundary audit does not check symlinks or special files, mutations to these paths could bypass detection
If evidence scope validation only checks field presence and not field values against VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES, invalid scopes could bypass validation

result.notes: All explorer claims verified independently. Evidence dispatch implements comprehensive boundary audit with snapshot-based mutation detection, multi-layered scope validation, and PRD readiness gating. Three-agent architecture (explorer/cross-checker/verifier) exists for high-risk demands. Tool access controls available at provider level. Verifier should confirm detection efficacy for edge cases like symlinks and validation completeness for all evidence paths.

agent_results.claim: All gathered evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness. No assumptions are presented as facts. All evidence paths are usable and verified. Unresolved missing data is correctly reflected as empty arrays.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() functions exist and implement hash-based mutation detection
evidence.why: Verifies explorer and cross-checker evidence for core boundary audit mechanism

evidence.summary: Runtime implementation shows boundary snapshots taken before/after execution and blockers created on violations
evidence.why: Confirms actual execution-time boundary audit that gates PRD readiness

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION requires scope field and VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines valid values
evidence.why: Verifies schema-level scope requirement enforcement

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() validates scope against VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES and creates EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blockers
evidence.why: Confirms validation layer that rejects evidence without proper scope classification

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() requires file locators for project-scoped evidence and creates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blockers
evidence.why: Verifies validation that prevents project facts without concrete file evidence

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() checks evidence agent results and calls scope/path validation functions
evidence.why: Confirms readiness validation loop that gates PRD on complete evidence

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() passes --allowedTools and --disallowedTools to Claude agent CLI
evidence.why: Verifies tool access controls that can restrict agent write capabilities

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross_checker, and verifier roles with protocols
evidence.why: Confirms three-agent architecture for cross-check validation

evidence.summary: execution_policy defines project_mutation as forbidden with allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Verifies policy-level boundary declaration controlling agent tool access

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() includes explicit boundary rules in agent instructions
evidence.why: Confirms agent-level boundary enforcement through prompt engineering

assumptions: Explorer and cross-checker assumptions about external systems (SHA-256 reliability, git ls-files accuracy, agent provider compliance) are properly separated from facts and do not affect core claim verification
risks: Documented risks about edge cases (symlinks, hash collisions, provider non-compliance) are appropriate and do not block PRD readiness for the core claim

result.notes: All evidence paths verified. Explorer and cross-checker results align with no conflicts. Assumptions properly separated from facts. Missing arrays correctly empty. Evidence validates claim that YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements multi-layered boundary audit with snapshot-based mutation detection, scoped evidence validation, and PRD readiness gating.
